1	# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing
2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.
35	
36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: the FULL-CORPUS gate for the slice must pass, not a
43	   fixture subset (see "Full-corpus gates" below). A narrow fixture suite that
44	   passes while the corpus gate fails is not acceptance.
45	4. Integration: the open side may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
46	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
47	   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
48	   with them (history retains them under GPL). Every integrator that touches a
49	   slice with a full-corpus gate MUST run that gate before declaring green.
50	
51	## Full-corpus gates (standard, not optional)
52	
53	Each slice has a fast oracle gate over the whole 419-file example corpus,
54	diffing the slice's output against the Haskell reference cache. These are the
55	sealed implementer's primary data source (run between build and the HS cache
56	every iteration) AND the integrator's mandatory acceptance check. Both take
57	`RS_PATH` (point at any build) and `ALLOWLIST` (subset while iterating).
58	
59	- Wellformedness: `scripts/wf_gate.sh` — runs the binary WITHOUT `--prove`
60	  (the wf block prints at theory-load, ~1s/file; full corpus ~72s at JOBS=6),
61	  extracts the `/* WARNING … */` block, diffs vs `scripts/.hs_file_cache`.
62	- Web: `scripts/web_parity.sh` — crawls HS and RS interactive servers per
63	  theory, semantic-diffs the response manifests vs `scripts/.web_hs_cache`.
64	- Full batch (proof output, slow ~30-60min): `scripts/corpus_file_diff.sh` —
65	  the ground-truth `--prove` byte diff; run at milestones, not every iteration.
66	
67	Once a slice's ported code is DELETED, the integrated module IS the sealed
68	reimplementation, so running the gate against the tree observes the sealed
69	code's own output vs the reference — legitimate feedback, not observation of
70	replaced code. For a not-yet-integrated slice the tree still holds the ported
71	code, so gate only the workspace build (via `RS_PATH`), never the stock tree.
72

26	  captures-only, no source tree.
27	- `oracle/captured_responses/` — the 81 captured crawl manifests (the sanctioned

44	(RS_PATH → your build, ALLOWLIST → a subset): full crawl, HS vs RS, **semantic**

1	# Required public API (interoperability surface) — producers-clean
2	
3	The clean crate must expose these entry points so the open side can wire it in
4	behind thin adapters that translate the live pre-computed prover values into the
5	crate's input shape (`fragment_inputs.rs`). Each function returns the exact
6	response-body bytes the corresponding fragment shows in the captures. Names are
7	indicative; the boundary that matters is "one entry point per observable
8	sub-target, each independently gate-checkable against the capture corpus".
9	
10	```rust
11	// ── R1: theory-view CENTER section fragment (`main/message` / `main/rules`
12	//    / `main/tactic` / `main/help`) — the whole response body, envelope
13	//    included. The deepest, most-reused leaf. ──
14	pub fn render_content_pane(pane: &ContentPane) -> String;
15	
16	//    …built on the shared HTML skin every producer reuses:
17	pub fn escape_text(s: &str) -> String;           // text → the observed entities
18	pub fn postprocess_lines(assembled: &str) -> String; // lines → breaks + indents
19	pub fn html_envelope(title: &str, html: &str) -> String;  // → the observed JSON
20	pub fn redirect_envelope(url: &str) -> String;
21	pub fn alert_envelope(msg: &str) -> String;
22	
23	// ── R2: proof-script WEST pane (the theory index left of every page) ──
24	pub fn render_proof_script(index: &ProofScriptPane) -> String;
25	
26	// ── R3: proof-tree + proof-method HTML (embedded in the west pane and the
27	//    per-path proof fragment) ──
28	pub fn render_proof_tree(index: u64, lemma: &str, tree: &ProofTree) -> String;
29	
30	// ── R4: welcome / index page (`/`) + housekeeping bodies ──
31	pub fn render_welcome(w: &Welcome) -> String;
32	
33	// ── R5: theory-path grammar (URL <-> structured path), pure, HTML-free ──
34	pub fn parse_path(raw: &str) -> Option<ThyPath>;
35	pub fn render_path(path: &ThyPath) -> Vec<String>;
36	```
37	
38	`ContentPane`, `HeadedBlock`, `Content`, `ProofScriptPane`, `NavItem`,
39	`LemmaEntry`, `ProofDisplay`, `ProofTree`, `Highlight`, `Welcome`, `Banner`,
40	`TheoryRow`, `ThyPath`: see `fragment_inputs.rs`.
41	
42	Every value these functions receive is pre-computed by the prover/pretty side
43	and only RENDERED here; the crate never re-derives prover content. All observed
44	bytes (tag skeletons, headings, titles, link targets, escaping, line breaks, the
45	envelope shape) are learned from the capture corpus + the live oracle — none are
46	specified in this file. Byte targets are captured OUTPUT only.

2	//
3	// The BEHAVIORAL input surface for the web fragment PRODUCERS: the shape of the
4	// pre-computed prover values each producer renders into a response-body
5	// fragment. Provided so the clean implementation can compile against a stable
6	// contract at integration time; the open side writes thin adapters from the
7	// live values into this shape.
8	//
9	// This is a BEHAVIORAL contract, not a transcription of any existing data
10	// model: every type/field below is named for what the producer OBSERVABLY does
11	// with it. No item here re-derives prover CONTENT — the text of a formula,
12	// rule, signature block, or proof method is computed elsewhere and reaches the
13	// producer as opaque `content`/`text` strings. The producer's whole job is the
14	// HTML/JSON SKIN the captures show around that content: tags, headings, links,
15	// escaping, line breaks, and the response envelope.
16	//
17	// The clean implementer MAY define an equivalent minimal model for unit testing
18	// (see workspace/producers-clean/src/model.rs) and only needs this exact shape

# Round-1 byte targets — sub-target R1 (theory-view CENTER section fragments:
# the `main/message`, `main/rules`, `main/tactic`, `main/help` JSON envelopes).
#
# Curated capture labels spanning the section-fragment variety (signature
# shapes, xor/multiset/nat, with/without restrictions, minimal→large). Each
# label is <theory-name>__<8-char capture-hash> so collision names (Joux,
# Scott, …) stay pinned to ONE capture. All four center fragments per label are
# materialized by fetch_targets.sh into targets/. These are the exact response
# bodies (JSON `{html,title}` envelope, decoded) a producer must reproduce.
#
# Format: <feature tags><TAB><label = name__hash8><TAB><note>
destr, fst/pair/snd, no-builtins-line	issue515__2b3b83d2	baseline: minimal signature, no builtins:, no restrictions
asym-enc, classic	NSPK3__642774eb	classic public-key: aenc/adec/pk/sign signature
bilinear, pmult/em	Joux__09829201	bilinear-pairing signature block
exp/dh	NAXOS_eCK__24416782	Diffie-Hellman exp signature
multiset, nat, subterm, xor	NumberSubtermTests__07246be7	%+ nat / multiset / subterm terms in the signature
xor, restrictions	CH07__09fc30cc	richest: xor signature + Restrictions section
xor, restrictions	CRXOR__e62b397a	xor + restrictions, compact
restrictions, aead/siv	PKCS11_siv__51ca69e4	restrictions + destructor-heavy signature
mac, signing	STS_MAC__1c375a9a	MAC + signing signature
restriction-only, counter	AC_counter__7f660677	no builtins: line, restrictions present, counter theory
large-signature, restrictions	WireGuard__2ad04d3b	large functions/equations block + restrictions

1	//! producers-clean: a clean-room reimplementation of the tamarin-prover
2	//! interactive web UI's fragment PRODUCERS — the code that renders pre-computed
3	//! prover values into the HTML/JSON response-body CONTENT that the already
4	//! clean-roomed dispatch layer serves. Derived purely from black-box
5	//! observation of captured responses + the live oracle. See ../../SPEC.md for
6	//! the observable boundary and the R1–R5 sub-target decomposition, and
7	//! workspace/BEHAVIOR.md for the behavioral spec you build up as you probe.
8	//!
9	//! SCAFFOLD ONLY. Every render entry point below is an UNIMPLEMENTED stub so
10	//! the crate compiles and the test harness runs from day one. Fill in bodies
11	//! one sub-target at a time, gating each against the corpus web-parity gate
12	//! with an ALLOWLIST (see round1/).
13	//!
14	//! Recommended order: R1 (center section fragments + the shared HTML skin) →
15	//! R2 (proof-script west pane) → R3 (proof-tree / method HTML) → R4 (welcome /
16	//! housekeeping) → R5 (theory-path grammar).
17	//!
18	//! In scope: pure-render producers — given pre-computed prover values, emit the
19	//! fragment bytes (tags, links, headings, escaping, line breaks, envelope).
20	//! OUT of scope (opaque input / other clusters): the pretty-printed content
21	//! text itself (formula / rule / signature / method text), the constraint
22	//! system and applicable-proof-methods panes, graph DOT/SVG, and the Rust-only
23	//! progressive-UI route.
24	
25	pub mod model;

1	//! Input model — the pre-computed prover VALUES the producers render.
2	//!
3	//! These are the crate's OWN, minimal, behavior-named types for unit testing
4	//! (build a value with Rust constructors, assert its rendered fragment equals an
5	//! observed capture). At integration the open side wires thin adapters from the
6	//! live typed-theory / proof-state values into this shape; see
7	//! ../../interface/ for the integration surface.
8	//!
9	//! Everything here is a value the producer only RENDERS. The text of a formula,
10	//! rule, signature, or proof method is computed elsewhere (the pretty / solver
11	//! side) and reaches the producer as opaque `content` strings — the producer
12	//! adds the HTML/JSON skin (tags, links, escaping, line breaks, envelope) the
13	//! captures show around it. No field here re-derives prover content.
14	
15	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
16	// R1 — theory-view CENTER section fragments (`main/<section>`)
17	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
18	
19	/// A content pane addressed by a `main/<section>` route, rendered as the
20	/// `{title, html}` response envelope. The pane is an ordered list of headed
21	/// blocks; the HTML skin (heading tags, monospace paragraph, the per-line
22	/// break/indent postprocess) is the producer's to add.
23	pub struct ContentPane {
24	    /// Envelope title (e.g. the message / rules / tactic pane's title text).
25	    pub title: String,
26	    /// Headed blocks, in document order.
27	    pub blocks: Vec<HeadedBlock>,
28	}
29	
30	/// One headed block: a heading line plus a monospace body.
31	pub struct HeadedBlock {
32	    /// Section heading text (a fixed vocabulary chosen per route).
33	    pub heading: String,
34	    /// Pre-rendered body content (opaque prover text, already line-structured;
35	    /// may carry its own inline emphasis to be skinned). Empty is meaningful.
36	    pub body: Content,
37	    /// Whether this block is emitted when its body is empty. Some sections are
38	    /// always present (empty paragraph); some vanish entirely when empty.
39	    pub keep_when_empty: bool,
40	}
41	
42	/// Opaque pre-rendered content: a sequence of logical lines plus, where known,
43	/// which spans are keyword / operator / comment emphasis (used only for the
44	/// byte-close HTML skin; the acceptance gate compares visible text, not skin).
45	pub struct Content {
46	    pub lines: Vec<String>,
47	}
48	
49	impl Content {
50	    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {
51	        self.lines.is_empty() || self.lines.iter().all(|l| l.is_empty())
52	    }
53	}

2	"""Slice fragment families out of the captured response corpus.
3	
4	The captured corpus (`oracle/captured_responses/*.hs.json`, the sanctioned

6	
7	    { base, capped, lemmas, log, manifest: { "<url>": {kind,status,body} } }
8	
9	`<url>` carries the literal token `#` where the server's version index goes;
10	`kind` is one of html | text | json | dot; a `json` body is itself the JSON
11	envelope string `{"html":..,"title":..}` (or `{"redirect":..}`/`{"alert":..}`).
12	
13	This helper lets the sealed room materialize the *exact response bytes* for one
14	fragment family without re-crawling — the byte targets a producer must
15	reproduce. It reads ONLY the captured corpus (no corpus `.spthy`, no source
16	tree); it labels each manifest by the theory name it advertises plus a short
17	content hash so name-collisions (Joux, Scott, …) stay distinct.
18	
19	Usage:
20	  extract_fragments.py list                       # manifests: label / name / kinds
21	  extract_fragments.py families                    # URL families + counts + kinds
22	  extract_fragments.py extract <family[,family2,...]> <outdir> [--only SUBSTR[,SUBSTR2,...]]
23	        # <family> is a URL skeleton, idx-agnostic; pass several comma-separated
24	        # to slice them all in ONE corpus pass, e.g.:
25	        #   overview   main/message   main/rules   main/tactic   main/help
26	        #   main/lemma   source   next   main/cases   main/proof
27	        # writes one file per (manifest, matching-url): <label>__<slug>.<ext>
28	        # json envelopes also emit a sibling .title file (the pane title).
29	  --only SUBSTR  restrict to manifests whose label CONTAINS any listed SUBSTR
30	                 (comma-separated). Omit to take every manifest.
31	
32	All output files are captured program OUTPUT (legitimate byte targets); the
33	`.meta.tsv` index records label -> theory-name -> capture-hash for traceability.
34	"""
35	import json
36	import os
37	import re
38	import sys
39	
40	HERE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
41	CORPUS = os.environ.get("CAPTURES", os.path.join(HERE, "captured_responses"))
42	
43	_IDX = re.compile(r"/thy/(trace|equiv)/[^/]+/")
44	
45	
46	def label_of(path, manifest):
47	    """theory-name + 8-char capture hash (collision-proof, capture-only)."""
48	    h = os.path.basename(path).split(".")[0][:8]
49	    name = None
50	    for url, r in manifest.items():
51	        if r.get("kind") == "html" and "/overview/" in url:
52	            m = re.search(r"<title>Theory:\s*([^<]*)</title>", r.get("body", ""))
53	            if m:
54	                name = m.group(1).strip()
55	                break
56	    if name is None:
57	        for url, r in manifest.items():
58	            if r.get("kind") == "text" and url.endswith("/source"):
59	                m = re.match(r"theory\s+(\S+)", r.get("body", ""))
60	                if m:
61	                    name = m.group(1)
62	                    break
63	    name = re.sub(r"[^A-Za-z0-9_.-]", "_", name or "UNKNOWN")
64	    return "%s__%s" % (name, h), name, h
65	
66	
67	def family_of(url):
68	    """idx-agnostic URL skeleton down to the handler section."""
69	    u = _IDX.sub("/thy/trace/#/", url)
70	    m = re.match(r"^/thy/trace/#/(main/[a-zA-Z]+|[a-zA-Z-]+)", u)
71	    if m:
72	        return m.group(1)
73	    return u.strip("/") or "root"
74	
75	
76	def slug(url):
77	    return re.sub(r"[^A-Za-z0-9]+", "_", _IDX.sub("/thy/trace/#/", url)).strip("_")
78	
79	
80	def manifests():
81	    for p in sorted(__import__("glob").glob(os.path.join(CORPUS, "*.hs.json"))):
82	        try:
83	            yield p, json.load(open(p))
84	        except Exception as e:  # noqa
85	            print("skip %s: %s" % (p, e), file=sys.stderr)
86	
87	
88	def cmd_list():
89	    for p, d in manifests():
90	        m = d.get("manifest", {})
91	        lbl, name, h = label_of(p, m)
92	        kinds = sorted({r.get("kind") for r in m.values()})
93	        print("%-40s  %-28s  urls=%-5d  kinds=%s" % (lbl, name, len(m), ",".join(kinds)))
94	
95	
96	def cmd_families():
97	    import collections
98	    fam = collections.Counter()
99	    famk = collections.defaultdict(set)
100	    for _, d in manifests():
101	        for url, r in d.get("manifest", {}).items():
102	            f = family_of(url)
103	            fam[f] += 1
104	            famk[f].add(r.get("kind"))
105	    for f, c in sorted(fam.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
106	        print("%6d  %-24s  %s" % (c, f, ",".join(sorted(famk[f]))))
107	
108	
109	def _write(outdir, base, body, title=None):
110	    os.makedirs(outdir, exist_ok=True)
111	    ext = "html" if ("<" in body or title is not None) else "txt"
112	    with open(os.path.join(outdir, base + "." + ext), "w") as fh:
113	        fh.write(body)
114	    if title is not None:
115	        with open(os.path.join(outdir, base + ".title"), "w") as fh:
116	            fh.write(title)
117	
118	
119	def cmd_extract(family, outdir, only=None):
120	    fams = set(f.strip() for f in family.split(",") if f.strip())
121	    subs = [s.strip() for s in only.split(",")] if only else None
122	    n = 0
123	    meta = []
124	    for p, d in manifests():
125	        m = d.get("manifest", {})
126	        lbl, name, h = label_of(p, m)
127	        if subs and not any(s in lbl for s in subs):
128	            continue
129	        for url, r in m.items():
130	            if family_of(url) not in fams:
131	                continue
132	            body = r.get("body", "")
133	            title = None
134	            if r.get("kind") == "json":
135	                try:
136	                    env = json.loads(body)
137	                except Exception:
138	                    env = None
139	                if isinstance(env, dict) and "html" in env:
140	                    title = env.get("title", "")
141	                    body = env["html"]
142	                # {redirect}/{alert} envelopes are left as raw JSON (no html key).
143	            base = "%s__%s" % (lbl, slug(url))
144	            _write(outdir, base, body, title)
145	            meta.append((base, name, h, url, r.get("kind"), r.get("status")))
146	            n += 1
147	    with open(os.path.join(outdir, ".meta.tsv"), "w") as fh:
148	        fh.write("target\ttheory\thash\turl\tkind\tstatus\n")
149	        for row in meta:
150	            fh.write("\t".join(str(x) for x in row) + "\n")
151	    print("wrote %d '%s' fragments to %s" % (n, family, outdir))
152	
153	
154	def main(argv):
155	    if not argv or argv[0] == "list":
156	        return cmd_list()
157	    if argv[0] == "families":
158	        return cmd_families()
159	    if argv[0] == "extract":
160	        family = argv[1]
161	        outdir = argv[2]
162	        only = None
163	        if "--only" in argv:
164	            only = argv[argv.index("--only") + 1]
165	        return cmd_extract(family, outdir, only)
166	    print(__doc__)
167	    return 2
168	
169	
170	if __name__ == "__main__":
171	    sys.exit(main(sys.argv[1:]) or 0)

555	Yesod default-layout error family.
556	
557	### 14.2 Theory-scoped additions
558	| handler | method | response |
559	|---------|--------|----------|
560	| `download/{file}` | GET | `200` `application/octet-stream`, **no** `Content-Disposition`; body == the `source` body verbatim (the `{file}` segment is decorative) |
561	| `reload` | POST | `200` JSON `{"redirect":"/thy/<kind>/<idx>/overview/help"}`; re-reads the theory **in place** at the same index |
562	| `get_and_append/{file}` | POST | `200` JSON `{"alert":"Appended lemmas to <path>"}` (the third envelope shape; path non-deterministic) |
563	| `edit/{verb}/{name}` | POST | structural edit (§13.3 / 14.6) |
564	
565	`source` and `message` are GET-only; a POST to them (or to `download`) is `405`.
566	
567	### 14.3 Version lifecycle — one global index namespace (spec item: state)
568	Every theory-version lives at a distinct index in **one** monotonically growing
569	namespace (`= max ever allocated + 1`), regardless of how it was produced:
570	* **Proof ops** (`method`, `diffMethod`, `autoprove*`) — new index, base retained.
571	* **Upload** (`POST /`) — new index off the **same** counter (NSLPK3 → 5 when
572	  1..4 existed). The response is the index page (200), **not** a redirect.
573	* **Structural edits** and **`reload`** — mutate in place at the same index; the
574	  counter is untouched and other versions stay resolvable (a proof op after a
575	  reload of v1 still got index 3 when 1,2 existed). Reload does **not** reset the
576	  counter or drop modified versions.
577	* Every allocated index stays resolvable forever (index-page window is a display
578	  cap only).
579	
580	### 14.4 Redirect caching headers
581	All `303 See Other` responses — `edit`/`add`/`delete` success, delete-not-found,
582	and the favicon redirect — carry `Cache-Control: no-cache, must-revalidate` and
583	`Expires: Thu, 01 Jan 1970 05:05:05 GMT` (modelled by `Response.no_cache`). The
584	JSON `{redirect}`/`{alert}` bodies (`method`, `autoprove`, `reload`,
585	`get_and_append`) are ordinary `200`s with no cache headers.
586	
587	### 14.5 Static content types
588	`/static/**` is a filesystem handler (chunked, **no** caching headers). Content
589	type is chosen by the last path segment's extension, **without** a `charset`:
590	`.css`→`text/css`, `.js`→`application/javascript`, `.png`→`image/png`,
591	`.ico`→`image/vnd.microsoft.icon`, no/unknown extension → `application/octet-stream`
592	(e.g. `/static/LICENSE`). A missing file yields the plain-text `File not found`
593	(not the dynamic HTML 404). `src/assets.rs::static_content_type`.
594	
595	### 14.6 Structural-edit branches (refines §13.3)
596	* **delete** `POST edit/delete/{name}`: lemma **found** → `303` →
597	  `overview/help` (removed in place); **not found** → `303` →
598	  `overview/delete/{name}` (theory unchanged). `delete_lemma` therefore returns
599	  `Option` (`None` = not found).
600	* **edit** `POST edit/edit/{name}`: success → `303` → `overview/edit/{name}`;
601	  parse failure **or unknown lemma** → `200` full-page edit form (theory
602	  unchanged).
603	* **add** `POST edit/add/{pos}`: success → `303` → `overview/add/{pos}`; failure
604	  → `200` add-form page.
605	* **method/diffMethod** failure: `200` JSON `{"alert":"Sorry, but the prover
606	  failed on the selected method!"}`, **no** version bump (`apply_method` /
607	  `apply_diff_method` return `Option`).
608	
609	### 14.7 Diff (observational-equivalence) mode — theory-kind `equiv`
610	Started with `--diff`; the index links use `/thy/equiv/…`. The overview shell is
611	the trace shell parameterised by `page::ShellKind::Equiv`: `<title>DiffTheory:
612	NAME`, `/thy/equiv/` links, and **no** Actions "Append modified lemmas" item
613	(MID/TAIL byte-identical to trace; reproduced byte-for-byte,
614	`equiv_overview_shell_byte_identical`). New handlers:
615	* `main/diffProof/{lemma}[/path…]` — JSON content pane (diff proof view).
616	* `main/diffMethod/{lemma}/{n}[/path…]` — proof op → `200` JSON
617	  `{"redirect":"/thy/equiv/<new>/overview/diffProof/{lemma}/{focus}"}` (new
618	  version) or the `{alert}` on failure.
619	* `main/diffrules` — JSON content pane.
620	* `autoproveDiff/{strategy}/{bound}/diffProof/{lemma}[/side…]` — **no** all-sol
621	  flag (unlike trace `autoprove`).
622	* `autoproveAll/{strategy}/{bound}` — autoprove every lemma.
623	`intdot`/`interactive-graph-def` tails in diff mode carry a `graph/` prefix
624	(opaque passthrough). autoproveDiff/autoproveAll **block the prover** on the
625	probe theory, so their exact redirect bytes are unobservable here; they are
626	dispatched as proof ops (new version) with the redirect modelled by analogy to
627	`diffMethod`/`autoprove` (documented gap).
628	
629	### 14.8 `del/path` and `verify` — the round-4 "absent" result was a PROBE-SHAPE ARTIFACT (resolved in Round 5)

12	#   hs_server.sh probe <port> <path>         # curl one route, print the body
13	#   hs_server.sh stop  <port>                # kill the server + free the port

21	# hs_server.sh passes `--port <n>` with a SPACE, which the prover's CmdArgs
22	# parser reads as the positional WORKDIR (`directory '<n>' does not exist`) and
23	# the server dies silently after a fixed `sleep 4`. This wrapper (a) uses
24	# `--port=<n>` (equals, like scripts/web_parity.sh), (b) POLLS for readiness

46	  if [ -n "$pid" ]; then

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Materialize the R1 fragment families' RAW response-body bytes.

Walks the sanctioned captured corpus (oracle/captured_responses/*.hs.json,
captured program OUTPUT) and writes, per manifest, the four center-fragment
responses' raw JSON-envelope bodies — the exact bytes the server sent — into
workspace/r1_raw/ as <theory>__<hash8>__<family>.raw. These are the byte
targets tests/corpus_sweep.rs replays the producer against (SPEC acceptance
ladder rung 2). Unlike oracle/extract_fragments.py this does NOT decode the
envelope: the sweep asserts envelope bytes too.

Reads ONLY the captured corpus. Labeling mirrors extract_fragments.py (theory
name advertised by the capture itself + 8-char capture hash).
"""
import glob
import json
import os
import re
import sys

HERE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
CORPUS = os.path.join(HERE, "..", "..", "oracle", "captured_responses")
OUT = os.path.join(HERE, "..", "r1_raw")

FAMILIES = ("main/message", "main/rules", "main/tactic", "main/help")
_IDX = re.compile(r"/thy/(trace|equiv)/[^/]+/")


def label_of(path, manifest):
    h = os.path.basename(path).split(".")[0][:8]
    name = None
    for url, r in manifest.items():
        if r.get("kind") == "html" and "/overview/" in url:
            m = re.search(r"<title>Theory:\s*([^<]*)</title>", r.get("body", ""))
            if m:
                name = m.group(1).strip()
                break
    if name is None:
        for url, r in manifest.items():
            if r.get("kind") == "text" and url.endswith("/source"):
                m = re.match(r"theory\s+(\S+)", r.get("body", ""))
                if m:
                    name = m.group(1)
                    break
    return "%s__%s" % (re.sub(r"[^A-Za-z0-9_.-]", "_", name or "UNKNOWN"), h)


def main():
    os.makedirs(OUT, exist_ok=True)
    n = 0
    rows = []
    for p in sorted(glob.glob(os.path.join(CORPUS, "*.hs.json"))):
        d = json.load(open(p))
        m = d.get("manifest", {})
        lbl = label_of(p, m)
        for url, r in m.items():
            u = _IDX.sub("/thy/trace/#/", url)
            fam = u[len("/thy/trace/#/"):] if u.startswith("/thy/trace/#/") else None
            if fam not in FAMILIES or r.get("kind") != "json":
                continue
            base = "%s__%s" % (lbl, fam.replace("/", "_"))
            with open(os.path.join(OUT, base + ".raw"), "w") as fh:
                fh.write(r["body"])
            rows.append((base, url, str(r.get("status"))))
            n += 1
    with open(os.path.join(OUT, ".meta.tsv"), "w") as fh:
        fh.write("target\turl\tstatus\n")
        for row in sorted(rows):
            fh.write("\t".join(row) + "\n")
    print("wrote %d raw fragments to %s" % (n, OUT))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())
